attributes.name: Strength
attributes.description: Governs Physical Power, which determines the damage bonus for physical attacks.

derived_stats.name: Physical Power
derived_stats.description: Raw physical power rating. Maps 1:1 from Strength.
derived_stats.mechanic: Your Physical Power equals your Strength, point for point. This rating is then converted to a damage bonus % via the Physical Power Bonus curve below.

derived_stats.name: Physical Power Bonus
derived_stats.description: Percentage modifier applied to physical damage. Derived from Physical Power rating via a scaling curve.
derived_stats.mechanic: Your weapon's base damage is multiplied by (1 + this bonus). For example, a Barbarian with 20 STR gets Physical Power 20, which gives roughly +5% bonus. A weapon dealing 30 base damage would deal 30 × 1.05 = 31.5. Below 15 STR the bonus goes negative, reducing your damage.

attributes.name: Vigor
attributes.description: Determines base maximum health and health recovery rate. Higher Vigor means a larger health pool and faster natural regeneration.

derived_stats.name: Max Health
derived_stats.description: Base maximum health points. Scales with Vigor via a nonlinear curve.
derived_stats.mechanic: This is your health pool before any gear bonuses. The curve has diminishing returns — early points of Vigor give more HP than later ones. A Fighter with 15 VIG gets 115 HP, while a Barbarian with 25 VIG gets 131.5 HP — only 16.5 more HP for 10 extra points.

derived_stats.name: Health Recovery
derived_stats.description: Modifier to health regeneration rate while resting.
derived_stats.mechanic: When you sit down to rest, your HP regenerates each tick. This modifier changes how fast: HP per tick = Base Recovery × (1 + this value). At +70% (Barbarian, 25 VIG), you heal 1.7× as fast. Below 15 VIG, recovery becomes slower — a Rogue with 6 VIG has -27%, making resting almost twice as slow.

attributes.name: Agility
attributes.description: Affects movement speed and action speed. Higher Agility increases move speed (with diminishing returns) and weapon swing/attack speed.

derived_stats.name: Move Speed Bonus
derived_stats.description: Flat bonus added to your base movement speed.
derived_stats.mechanic: Your total Move Speed = Base (300) + this bonus, but hard capped at 330. A Rogue with 25 AGI gets +5 bonus = 305 move speed. A Ranger with 20 AGI gets +2.5 = 302.5. Gear can push you further toward the 330 cap. Below 15 AGI you get a penalty — at 0 AGI you'd lose 10 move speed.

derived_stats.name: Action Speed (from Agility)
derived_stats.description: Action speed modifier from Agility. Contributes to your total Action Speed alongside Dexterity.
derived_stats.mechanic: Action Speed controls how fast you swing weapons, draw bows, and block. Both Agility and Dexterity contribute, but the game describes Agility's contribution as 'slight' while Dexterity is the primary driver. The formula is 0.25 × Agility + 0.75 × Dexterity, meaning Dexterity has 3× more influence per point. Both stats use the same curve (shown below).

attributes.name: Dexterity
attributes.description: Affects action speed, manual dexterity (instrument playing for Bard), and item equip speed for weapons and armor.

derived_stats.name: Action Speed (from Dexterity)
derived_stats.description: Action speed modifier from Dexterity. The dominant contributor to total Action Speed.
derived_stats.mechanic: Dexterity is the primary Action Speed stat — the game itself says it directly 'increases your action speed' while Agility only 'slightly' increases it. The formula is 0.25 × Agility + 0.75 × Dexterity, both fed into this curve. Prioritize Dexterity when optimizing attack speed.

derived_stats.name: Manual Dexterity
derived_stats.description: Affects Bard instrument playing speed.
derived_stats.mechanic: Bard-specific stat that controls how quickly you can play instruments. Scales well up to about 45 DEX, then heavily plateaus — going from 45 to 100 DEX barely improves it. Most classes won't notice this stat.

derived_stats.name: Item Equip Speed
derived_stats.description: Speed modifier for equipping armor and ranged weapons.
derived_stats.mechanic: Controls how long it takes to put on armor pieces and equip ranged weapons during a match. At very low DEX (0-1), equipping is nearly frozen (-95%). At 15 DEX it's normal speed. Higher DEX dramatically speeds it up — at 35 DEX you equip at 2× normal speed. This matters most for classes that swap gear mid-fight.

attributes.name: Will
attributes.description: The primary stat for magic users. Governs Magical Power, Magic Resistance, buff/debuff durations, and magical interaction speed (shrines, portals).

derived_stats.name: Magical Power
derived_stats.description: Raw magical power rating. Maps 1:1 from Will.
derived_stats.mechanic: Works exactly like Physical Power but for spells. Your Magical Power equals your Will stat point for point. This rating then converts to a damage bonus % via the Magical Power Bonus curve.

derived_stats.name: Magical Power Bonus
derived_stats.description: Percentage modifier applied to magical/spell damage.
derived_stats.mechanic: Spell damage is multiplied by (1 + this bonus). Uses the same curve shape as Physical Power Bonus. A Sorcerer with 25 WIL gets +10% spell damage, while a Rogue with 10 WIL takes a -8% penalty to any magical damage they deal.

derived_stats.name: Magic Resistance
derived_stats.description: Magic resistance rating that reduces incoming spell damage.
derived_stats.mechanic: This rating feeds into the Magical Damage Reduction curve (see Defense tab). Higher Will gives higher resistance, which means less spell damage taken. A Sorcerer with 25 WIL gets 54 Magic Resistance. This is separate from Armor Rating — you can be well-protected against spells but vulnerable to weapons, or vice versa.

derived_stats.name: Buff Duration
derived_stats.description: Modifier to how long beneficial effects (buffs) last on you.
derived_stats.mechanic: Buff duration = Base Duration × (1 + this modifier). A Cleric with 23 WIL gets about +9.6% — their Protection spell lasts ~10% longer. Uses the same curve shape as Physical Power Bonus. Below 15 WIL, your buffs expire faster.

derived_stats.name: Debuff Duration
derived_stats.description: Modifier to how long negative effects (debuffs) last on you.
derived_stats.mechanic: This curve is inverted — positive values mean debuffs last LONGER. At low Will, debuffs are devastating: a Rogue with 10 WIL has +12.4%, meaning poison/slow effects last ~12% longer. At high Will, debuffs are shortened. The penalty is extreme at 0 Will — debuffs would last 5× normal duration.

derived_stats.name: Magical Interaction Speed
derived_stats.description: Speed modifier for magical interactions like opening shrines and portals.
derived_stats.mechanic: Controls how quickly you can activate shrines, open portals, and interact with magical objects. Uses the same curve as Health Recovery. A Wizard with 20 WIL opens shrines about 35% faster than baseline.

attributes.name: Knowledge
attributes.description: Governs spell casting speed, memory capacity (how many spells can be equipped), and memory recovery rate (spell recharge speed).

derived_stats.name: Spell Casting Speed
derived_stats.description: Modifier to how fast you cast spells.
derived_stats.mechanic: Cast time = Base Cast Time / (1 + this modifier). A Wizard with 25 KNO gets +21% — a spell with 2s base cast time takes 2 / 1.21 = 1.65s. This is critical for spell classes. Barbarian at 5 KNO gets -35%, making their rare spell casts painfully slow.

derived_stats.name: Memory Capacity
derived_stats.description: Determines how many spell slots are available.
derived_stats.mechanic: Each spell has a memory cost. Your Memory Capacity limits which spells you can equip. Below 7 Knowledge you get zero capacity and cannot equip any spells. Above that, each point gives roughly 1 more capacity. A Wizard with 25 KNO gets 19 capacity, enough for several spells.

derived_stats.name: Memory Recovery
derived_stats.description: Multiplier for how fast spent spell slots recharge.
derived_stats.mechanic: When you rest at a campfire or meditate, your spent spell slots recover. This multiplier speeds that up. Unlike Health Recovery, this never goes negative — even at 0 Knowledge you still recover at 0.43× rate. At 25 KNO you get about 0.85× rate, and it climbs steeply above 30.

attributes.name: Resourcefulness
attributes.description: Governs regular interaction speed (opening chests, doors), cooldown reduction for skills/spells, and persuasiveness (Bard buff/debuff modifier).

derived_stats.name: Regular Interaction Speed
derived_stats.description: Speed modifier for non-magical interactions like opening chests, doors, and reviving.
derived_stats.mechanic: Controls how fast you open chests, unlock doors, disarm traps, and revive teammates. A Rogue with 25 RES gets +52% — they open chests roughly 1.5× faster than a Fighter. Combined with Dexterity's contribution, Rogues are the fastest looters.

derived_stats.name: Cooldown Reduction
derived_stats.description: Reduces the cooldown time on your skills and spells.
derived_stats.mechanic: Effective Cooldown = Base Cooldown × (1 - this value). A Rogue with 25 RES gets about +20% CDR — a 30s cooldown becomes 30 × 0.8 = 24s. This is hard capped at 65% reduction, so cooldowns can never be reduced below 35% of their base value.

derived_stats.name: Persuasiveness
derived_stats.description: Affects Bard buff/debuff effectiveness.
derived_stats.mechanic: Bard-only stat. Increases the potency of your songs' buff and debuff effects on allies and enemies. Scales linearly up to about 35 RES, then hits diminishing returns and eventually plateaus. Most non-Bard classes can ignore this stat.

armor_rating.name: Physical Damage Reduction
armor_rating.description: Converts Armor Rating into percentage physical damage reduction. Subject to heavy diminishing returns at high values.

magic_resistance.name: Magical Damage Reduction
magic_resistance.description: Converts Magic Resistance rating into percentage magical damage reduction. Same diminishing-returns curve shape as physical.

luck_grades.description: Luck modifies drop rate weights per rarity tier. Lower grades (common) decrease with Luck, higher grades (rare/legendary) increase.

max_oxygen.name: Max Oxygen
max_oxygen.description: Maximum breath duration underwater. Scales linearly with the stat.

rigidity.name: Freeze Threshold
rigidity.description: Maps freeze buildup (0-1) to freeze intensity. Has a sharp ramp that creates a 'freeze threshold' behavior.

primitive_calc.name: True Damage Scaling
primitive_calc.description: Used for true damage calculations. Shows extreme diminishing returns above 50.
